title: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix
pattern: Binary Search on Answer (over Value Range)
coreIdea: Find the kth smallest element in a row-and-column sorted matrix by binary searching over the value range (not indices), using a counting function that leverages the matrix's sorted structure to count elements <= a candidate value in O(rows+cols).
recognitionCues: matrix sorted along both rows and columns
asks for kth smallest without fully flattening and sorting
binary search on answer applicable since 'count of elements <= x' is monotonic
approach: Binary search between matrix[0][0] and matrix[n-1][n-1]. For each candidate mid value, count how many matrix elements are <= mid by walking from the bottom-left corner (or top-right), moving right when the value is <= mid (counting that column's contribution) and up otherwise. If count >= k, search lower half; else search upper half.
